content.content:
title:	BTRFS missing from idMapMount filesystem blacklist — EOVERFLOW on bind mounts
state:	CLOSED
author:	thieso2
labels:	
comments:	0
assignees:	
projects:	
milestone:	
number:	12
--
## Summary

Sysbox's ID-mapped mount filesystem blacklist (`sysbox-libs/idMap/idMapMount.go:42`) does not include `BTRFS_SUPER_MAGIC`. This causes EOVERFLOW errors when bind-mounting directories from a BTRFS filesystem into Sysbox containers on kernels ≥5.19 (where shiftfs is unavailable and ID-mapped mounts are used instead).

## Symptoms

Inside a Sysbox container with a BTRFS bind mount:

```
chmod: /home/thies: Value too large for defined data type
touch: /home/thies/test: Permission denied
```

- `stat` shows bind-mounted dirs as `nobody:nobody`
- `chmod`, `chown`, `touch`, and file creation all fail with EOVERFLOW
- Containers with home-directory bind mounts enter a restart loop

## Root Cause

On kernel 6.18 (no shiftfs module), Sysbox auto-detects ID-mapped mount support and applies `mount_setattr()` with `MOUNT_ATTR_IDMAP` to bind mounts. The blacklist in `idMapMountFsBlackList` only contains:

```go
var idMapMountFsBlackList = []int64{
    unix.OVERLAYFS_SUPER_MAGIC,
    0x65735546,  // FUSE_SUPER_MAGIC
    0x6a656a63,  // FAKEOWNER
}
```

BTRFS (`0x9123683E`) is missing. Upstream issue [nestybox/sysbox#535](https://github.com/nestybox/sysbox/issues/535) documents that BTRFS is unsupported for ID-mapped mounts, but the blacklist was never updated.


content.text:
Confirmed — issue #12 is closed. Then with working BTRFS idmap, the big `chown -R /home/$USERNAME` (and the `chown -R /persisted`, and probably the top-level `chmod 777` too) are all pure overhead — sysbox remaps UIDs transparently now, so files appear correctly owned inside the container without any host-side fixup. The whole block at entrypoint.sh:32-56 is a workaround for a bug that no longer exists.

Want me to:
1. **Strip the chown/chmod block** from `entrypoint.sh` — should shave the bulk of the start time for users with big homes.
2. **Switch `ssh-keygen -A` to ed25519-only** — saves the RSA keygen (the slow one).
3. **Update the CLAUDE.md "BTRFS Support" section** — it still says "Falls back gracefully to regular directories if BTRFS operations fail" which is fine, but the `thieso2/sysbox#12` workaround comment should go.

Safe fallback if we're nervous: keep a cheap `stat -c %u` guard that does the chown only when ownership actually looks wrong — handles old sysbox versions and costs one syscall on the happy path.

Should I do all three, or just (1)+(2) and leave the comment cleanup for later?

input.old_string:
# Set correct ownership and permissions on the home directory.
# chown -R covers .ssh, .local, and anything else created above.
# The host ensures 777 on the mount point before container start (via sudo
# chmod in SandboxManager#prepare_bind_mount), so the sandbox user can always
# write ~/.Xauthority, .ssh, etc.
#
# On BTRFS bind mounts with Sysbox ID-mapped mounts, chmod/chown may fail
# with EOVERFLOW ("Value too large for defined data type"). This is a known
# Sysbox bug (thieso2/sysbox#12). Log the error visibly but don't crash —
# the host has already set 777 on the mount point.
if ! chown -R "$USERNAME:$USERNAME" "/home/$USERNAME" 2>/tmp/chown-home.err; then
    echo "WARNING: chown /home/$USERNAME failed: $(cat /tmp/chown-home.err | head -1)" >&2
    echo "  This is likely BTRFS + Sysbox ID-mapped mount issue (thieso2/sysbox#12)" >&2
fi
if ! chmod 777 "/home/$USERNAME" 2>/tmp/chmod-home.err; then
    echo "WARNING: chmod /home/$USERNAME failed: $(cat /tmp/chmod-home.err | head -1)" >&2
    echo "  This is likely BTRFS + Sysbox ID-mapped mount issue (thieso2/sysbox#12)" >&2
fi

# Fix ownership on persisted data volume. Multiple sandboxes can share the
# same user's data path, each with a different Sysbox UID mapping. Run in
# background to avoid delaying SSH readiness.
if [ -d /persisted ]; then
    (chown -R "$USERNAME:$USERNAME" /persisted 2>/dev/null || true) &
fi
input.new_string:
# Ownership of bind-mounted $HOME and /persisted is handled by Sysbox's
# ID-mapped mounts — the host UID is remapped transparently so files appear
# correctly owned inside the container. No recursive chown needed on the
# happy path. A cheap stat guard falls back to the old recursive chown if
# the top-level dir still looks wrong (e.g. pre-idmap sysbox versions).
USER_UID="$(id -u "$USERNAME")"
if [ "$(stat -c %u "/home/$USERNAME" 2>/dev/null)" != "$USER_UID" ]; then
    (chown -R "$USERNAME:$USERNAME" "/home/$USERNAME" 2>/dev/null || true) &
fi
if [ -d /persisted ] && [ "$(stat -c %u /persisted 2>/dev/null)" != "$USER_UID" ]; then
    (chown -R "$USERNAME:$USERNAME" /persisted 2>/dev/null || true) &
fi

content.text: Now the host key generation — skip RSA, keep ed25519.

input.old_string:
# Generate SSH host keys if missing

input.new_string:
# Generate SSH host keys if missing. Only ed25519 — modern clients prefer
# it and skipping RSA saves 1-3s of keygen on every container start.
# `ssh-keygen -A` generates all key types; we generate a single one instead.
if [ ! -f /etc/ssh/ssh_host_ed25519_key ]; then
    ssh-keygen -q -t ed25519 -f /etc/ssh/ssh_host_ed25519_key -N "" < /dev/null
fi

content.content:
79:### BTRFS Support
81:Sandcastle automatically detects and uses BTRFS subvolumes when the filesystem supports it:
84:- Detects if `$SANDCASTLE_HOME` is on BTRFS
96:- Falls back gracefully to regular directories if BTRFS operations fail
99:**Security**: Passwordless sudo limited to BTRFS subvolume commands only (`/etc/sudoers.d/sandcastle`).

content.text:
All three fixes applied. Summary of what changed:

**`images/sandbox/entrypoint.sh`**
- Dropped the unconditional recursive `chown -R /home/$USERNAME` and the `chmod 777` on top of it. Replaced with a cheap `stat -c %u` guard — one syscall on the happy path; if UIDs ever look wrong (old sysbox), the chown runs in the background so it still doesn't block sshd.
- Same guard pattern for `/persisted`.
- `ssh-keygen -A` → ed25519-only keygen, and only when the key file is missing. Saves the ~1-3s RSA generation every start.

**`images/sandbox/sc-install-brew.sh`** (new) + **Dockerfile** wiring — one-shot Linuxbrew installer with `--opt` / `--prefix` flags, appends a guarded activation snippet to `~/.bashrc` so a missing prefix can't break future shells.

**`app/views/pages/guide.html.erb`** — new section 17 documenting `sc-install-brew` with the persist-vs-ephemeral tradeoff and the bottles caveat.

CLAUDE.md's BTRFS section didn't reference `sysbox#12` directly (only the entrypoint comments did), so nothing to prune there.

Next time you rebuild the sandbox image, a fresh container should go from `docker run` to SSH-ready in well under a second on the common path — no more walking a node_modules-heavy home, no more RSA keygen.

content.text:
Hitting blockers I should report before going further:

1. `sandman` doesn't resolve on this host — no Tailscale up locally, no `~/.ssh/config` entry.
2. Trying the Tailscale IP from CLAUDE.md (`100.106.185.92`) directly: `Permission denied` + no `ssh-askpass` — it's burning through my loaded keys and hitting `MaxAuthTries` before prompting.

Before I push on this, two checks from you:

- **Connection**: is Tailscale supposed to be up on this box? Or should I use the public hostname (presumably `sandman.sandcastle.rocks` or similar)? If there's a jumphost / key path I should add to `~/.ssh/config`, tell me.
- **Scope**: "create an Incus VM on sandman, install a fresh Sandcastle" is a solid ~30 min of provisioning (Ubuntu image, bootstrap script, Docker+Sysbox, first sandbox pull, DB init) on your prod host. And my changes live on uncommitted `main` locally — to test them I'd need to either push a branch and build the sandbox image in CI, or build locally and push to a registry the VM can pull from.

A much cheaper path for validating this specific fix: build the modified sandbox image right here, run it as a plain Docker container with a bind-mounted "populated" home, and measure time-to-SSH-ready + verify ed25519-only host keys + verify `sc-install-brew` exists. No sandman, no VM, ~2 min. Says nothing about the full Sandcastle install flow, but tells you definitively whether the entrypoint changes work.

Which do you want — fix SSH to sandman and do the full VM build, or local container validation, or both?

input.description: Check installer pull logic during install

content.content:
3860:   # so dockyard's own iptables rules handle NAT/forwarding automatically.
3861:   # No separate sandcastle-nat service needed.
3862:   if $DOCKER network inspect sandcastle-web &>/dev/null; then
3863:     ok "sandcastle-web network exists"
3864:   else
3865:     $DOCKER network create sandcastle-web >/dev/null
3866:     ok "sandcastle-web network created"
3867:   fi
3868: 
3869:   # ── Pull images ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3870: 
3871:   info "Pulling images..."
3872:   $DOCKER pull "$APP_IMAGE" &
3873:   $DOCKER pull "$SANDBOX_IMAGE" &
3874:   $DOCKER pull traefik:v3.6 &
3875:   $DOCKER pull busybox:latest &
3876:   wait
3877:   ok "Images pulled"
3878: 
3879:   # ── Write PostgreSQL init script ──────────────────────────────────────────
3880: 
3881:   mkdir -p "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc/postgres"
3882:   cat > "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc/postgres/init-databases.sh" <<'INITDB'
3883: #!/bin/bash
3884: set -e
3885: 
3886: # Create additional databases needed by Solid Cache, Queue, Cable, and Errors.
3887: # The primary database (sandcastle_production) is created automatically
3888: # by POSTGRES_DB, but the Solid* gems each need their own database.
3889: 
3890: for db in sandcastle_production_cache sandcastle_production_queue sandcastle_production_cable sandcastle_production_errors; do
3891:   echo "Creating database: $db"
3892:   psql -v ON_ERROR_STOP=1 --username "$POSTGRES_USER" --dbname "$POSTGRES_DB" <<-EOSQL
3893:     SELECT 'CREATE DATABASE $db OWNER $POSTGRES_USER'
3894:     WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT FROM pg_database WHERE datname = '$db')\gexec
3895: EOSQL
3896: done
3897: INITDB
3898:   chmod +x "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc/postgres/init-databases.sh"
3899:   wrote "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc/postgres/init-databases.sh"
3900:

content.text:
I screwed up. My earlier `pkill -9 -f ssh` was too broad — it killed the forwarded ssh-agent and the `ssh-agent-switcher` daemon that serves `/tmp/ssh-agent.thies` on this host. I now have no usable key to authenticate to sandman with, and there are no private key files on this machine (only the forwarded agent, which is gone).

Current state:
- VM not created yet.
- No state changed on sandman — only `/tmp/sandcastle-src.tgz` (the 7.4MB source tarball) and `/tmp/sc-fix-test-cloud-init.yaml` landed there earlier. Easy cleanup if you want.
- My tmp key is at `/tmp/sc-fix-test-key{,.pub}` locally (unused).

To continue I need you to re-establish the SSH session to this host so the forwarded agent comes back. A clean reconnect should restore `/tmp/ssh-agent.thies`; `sc-tmux` would relaunch the switcher daemon too.

Once the agent is back, I'll be much more disciplined:
- Reuse a single `ControlMaster` connection for everything to avoid churn.
- Do the bulk of the work via a single SSH with a heredoc / script, not dozens of round-trips.
- Never `pkill ssh` again — I'll target specific PIDs.

Say the word when you've reconnected and I'll resume.

content.text: Agent is back. Opening a ControlMaster to sandman and doing all work through that.

input.command:
mkdir -p ~/.ssh/cm
cat > ~/.ssh/sandman-config <<'EOF'
Host sandman
    HostName 100.100.218.64
    User thies
    ControlMaster auto
    ControlPath ~/.ssh/cm/sandman.sock
    ControlPersist 600
    ServerAliveInterval 30
    IdentitiesOnly no
EOF
ssh -F ~/.ssh/sandman-config sandman 'hostname; incus --version'

input.command:
SSH="ssh -F $HOME/.ssh/sandman-config sandman"
SCP="scp -F $HOME/.ssh/sandman-config"
PUBKEY=$(cat /tmp/sc-fix-test-key.pub)
cat > /tmp/sc-fix-test-cloud-init.yaml <<EOF
#cloud-config
users:
  - name: ubuntu
    sudo: ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL
    shell: /bin/bash
    ssh_authorized_keys:
      - ${PUBKEY}
# Wait for SSH to be ready before cloud-init reports "done"
runcmd:
  - [systemctl, enable, --now, ssh]
package_update: false
package_upgrade: false
EOF
$SCP /tmp/sc-fix-test-cloud-init.yaml sandman:/tmp/sc-fix-test-cloud-init.yaml
$SSH 'ls -lh /tmp/sc-fix-test-cloud-init.yaml && head -10 /tmp/sc-fix-test-cloud-init.yaml'

content.subject: Launch sc-fix-test VM on sandman

content.subject: Install Docker + Sysbox in VM
content.description: Install Docker CE and Sysbox runtime so sandcastle sandboxes can run

content.subject: Ship source + run Sandcastle installer
content.description: Transfer tarball via sandman, extract, run installer.sh install

content.subject: Build sandbox image with my changes
content.description: Rebuild ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest from local source so the fix is in the running image

content.subject: Create sandbox and validate fix
content.description: Measure time-to-SSH-ready, verify ed25519-only host keys, verify no recursive chown, verify sc-install-brew present

SSH="ssh -F $HOME/.ssh/sandman-config sandman"
$SSH 'incus exec sc-fix-test -- bash -c "groups ubuntu; cat /etc/sudoers.d/*ubuntu* 2>/dev/null; cat /etc/sudoers.d/90-cloud-init-users 2>/dev/null"'

cat >> ~/.ssh/sandman-config <<'EOF'

Host sc-fix-test
    HostName 10.146.154.252
    User ubuntu
    ProxyJump sandman
    IdentityFile /tmp/sc-fix-test-key
    IdentitiesOnly yes
    StrictHostKeyChecking accept-new
    UserKnownHostsFile /tmp/sc-fix-test-known_hosts
    ControlMaster auto
    ControlPath ~/.ssh/cm/sc-fix-test.sock
    ControlPersist 600
EOF
ssh -F ~/.ssh/sandman-config sc-fix-test 'hostname; uname -a; df -h / | tail -1'

content.content:
sandcastle-bootstrap.sh
---
#!/bin/bash
# Sandcastle server bootstrap script
# Tested on Ubuntu 24.04 (Hetzner root server)
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Sandcastle Server Bootstrap ==="

# 1. Install Docker
if ! command -v docker &>/dev/null; then
    echo "Installing Docker..."
    curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com | sh
    systemctl enable --now docker
else
    echo "Docker already installed."
fi

# 2. Install Sysbox
if ! docker info --format '{{json .Runtimes}}' | grep -q sysbox; then
    echo "Installing Sysbox..."
    SYSBOX_VERSION="0.6.6"
    ARCH=$(dpkg --print-architecture)
    wget -q "https://downloads.nestybox.com/sysbox/releases/v${SYSBOX_VERSION}/sysbox-ce_${SYSBOX_VERSION}-0.linux_${ARCH}.deb" \
        -O /tmp/sysbox.deb
    apt-get install -y /tmp/sysbox.deb
    rm /tmp/sysbox.deb
    systemctl restart docker
else
    echo "Sysbox already installed."
fi

# 3. Configure UFW
echo "Configuring firewall..."
ufw --force reset
ufw default deny incoming
ufw default allow outgoing
ufw allow 22/tcp       # SSH (host)
ufw allow 80/tcp       # HTTP (Traefik — ACME + redirect)
ufw allow 443/tcp      # HTTPS (Traefik)
ufw allow 2201:2299/tcp # Sandbox SSH ports
ufw --force enable

# 1. Install Docker

else

fi

# 2. Install Sysbox
if ! docker info --format '{{json .Runtimes}}' | grep -q sysbox; then

content.subject: Launch sc-fix-test VM on sandman
content.description: Create Ubuntu 25.10 VM via incus with 4 CPU, 8GB RAM, 30GB disk

content.subject: Install Docker + Sysbox in VM
content.description: Install Docker CE and Sysbox runtime so sandcastle sandboxes can run

content.subject: Ship source + run Sandcastle installer
content.description: Transfer tarball via sandman, extract, run installer.sh install

content.subject: Build sandbox image with my changes
content.description: Rebuild ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest from local source so the fix is in the running image

content.subject: Create sandbox and validate fix
content.description: Measure time-to-SSH-ready, verify ed25519-only host keys, verify no recursive chown, verify sc-install-brew present

content.content:
# ═══ load_env ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

load_env() {
  local script_dir
  script_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
  local home="${SANDCASTLE_HOME:-/sandcastle}"

  LOADED_ENV_FILE=""
  if [ -n "${SANDCASTLE_ENV:-}" ]; then
    [ ! -f "$SANDCASTLE_ENV" ] && die "Env file not found: $SANDCASTLE_ENV"
    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$(cd "$(dirname "$SANDCASTLE_ENV")" && pwd)/$(basename "$SANDCASTLE_ENV")"
  elif [ -f "./sandcastle.env" ]; then
    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$(pwd)/sandcastle.env"
  elif [ -f "$script_dir/sandcastle.env" ]; then
    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$script_dir/sandcastle.env"
  elif [ -f "$home/etc/sandcastle.env" ]; then
    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$home/etc/sandcastle.env"
  fi

  if [ -n "$LOADED_ENV_FILE" ]; then
    set -a
    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
    source "$LOADED_ENV_FILE"
    set +a
    info "Loaded $LOADED_ENV_FILE"
  fi
}

# ═══ derive_vars ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

derive_vars() {
  SANDCASTLE_HOME="${SANDCASTLE_HOME:-/sandcastle}"
  APP_IMAGE="${APP_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle:latest}"
  SANDBOX_IMAGE="${SANDBOX_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest}"
  SANDCASTLE_USER="${SANDCASTLE_USER:-sandcastle}"
  SANDCASTLE_GROUP="${SANDCASTLE_GROUP:-$SANDCASTLE_USER}"
  SANDCASTLE_UID="${SANDCASTLE_UID:-220568}"
  SANDCASTLE_GID="${SANDCASTLE_GID:-220568}"
  SANDCASTLE_HTTP_PORT="${SANDCASTLE_HTTP_PORT:-80}"
  SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT="${SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT:-443}"
  TCP_PORT_MIN="${SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MIN:-3000}"
  TCP_PORT_MAX="${SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MAX:-3099}"
  SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE="${SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE:-selfsigned}"
  SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER="${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER:-admin}"

  DOCKYARD_ROOT="${DOCKYARD_ROOT:-$SANDCASTLE_HOME/dockyard}"
  DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX="${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX:-sc_}"

  # Single RFC 1918 /16 from which all Sandcastle Docker networks are carved.
  # Must be a private range (10.x.x.x, 172.16-31.x.x, or 192.168.x.x).
  SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET="${SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET:-10.89.0.0/16}"
  _priv_base="${SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET%%/*}"        # e.g. "10.89.0.0"
  _priv_prefix="${_priv_base%.*.*}"                  # e.g. "10.89"
  DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR="${DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR:-${_priv_prefix}.0.1/24}"
  DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR="${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR:-${_priv_prefix}.0.0/24}"
  DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE="${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE:-${SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET}}"
  DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE="${DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE:-24}"

  DOCKER_SOCK="${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/run/docker.sock"
  DOCKER="${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/bin/docker"

  ARCH=$(dpkg --print-architecture 2>/dev/null || echo "amd64")

  # Resolve password from file if needed
  if [ -z "${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD:-}" ] && [ -n "${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD_FILE:-}" ]; then
    [ ! -f "$SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD_FILE" ] && die "Password file not found: $SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD_FILE"
    SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD="$(cat "$SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD_FILE")"
  fi
}

# ═══ setup_ssh_keys ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Copy deploying user's SSH keys to sandcastle user

setup_ssh_keys() {
  local deploying_user="${SUDO_USER:-}"

  if [ -z "$deploying_user" ]; then
    warn "No SUDO_USER found — skipping SSH key setup"
    return
  fi

  local source_keys="/home/${deploying_user}/.ssh/authorized_keys"
  local target_dir="${SANDCASTLE_HOME}/.ssh"
  local target_keys="${target_dir}/authorized_keys"

  if [ ! -f "$source_keys" ]; then
    warn "No SSH keys found for user '${deploying_user}' — skipping SSH key setup"
    return
  fi

  info "Setting up SSH keys for '${SANDCASTLE_USER}'..."

content.content:
cmd_gen_env() {
    local NOCHECK=false
    for arg in "$@"; do
        case "$arg" in
            --nocheck)  NOCHECK=true ;;
            -h|--help)  gen_env_usage ;;
            --*)        echo "Unknown option: $arg" >&2; gen_env_usage ;;
        esac
    done

    # Determine output file
    local out_file="${DOCKYARD_ENV:-./dockyard.env}"
    if [ -f "$out_file" ]; then
        echo "Error: ${out_file} already exists. Remove it first or set DOCKYARD_ENV to a different path." >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    # Apply env var overrides or defaults
    local root="${DOCKYARD_ROOT:-/dockyard}"
    local prefix="${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX:-dy_}"
    local pool_size="${DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE:-24}"

    # Generate random networks if not provided via env
    local bridge_cidr="${DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR:-}"
    local fixed_cidr="${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR:-}"
    local pool_base="${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE:-}"

    if [ -z "$bridge_cidr" ] || [ -z "$fixed_cidr" ] || [ -z "$pool_base" ]; then
        local attempts=0
        local max_attempts=10

        while [ $attempts -lt $max_attempts ]; do
            attempts=$((attempts + 1))

            # Random /24 from 172.16.0.0/12 for bridge
            local b2=$(( RANDOM % 16 + 16 ))   # 16-31
            local b3=$(( RANDOM % 256 ))        # 0-255
            bridge_cidr="${DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR:-172.${b2}.${b3}.1/24}"
            fixed_cidr="${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR:-172.${b2}.${b3}.0/24}"

            # Random /16 from 172.16.0.0/12 for pool (different second octet)
            local p2=$(( RANDOM % 16 + 16 ))    # 16-31
            while [ "$p2" -eq "$b2" ]; do
                p2=$(( RANDOM % 16 + 16 ))
            done
            pool_base="${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE:-172.${p2}.0.0/16}"

            if [ "$NOCHECK" = true ]; then
                break
            fi

            local env_dir
            env_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${out_file}")" 2>/dev/null && pwd)" || env_dir=""
            if check_subnet_conflict "$fixed_cidr" "$pool_base" 2>/dev/null && \
               check_sibling_conflict "$fixed_cidr" "$pool_base" "$env_dir" 2>/dev/null; then
                break
            fi

            [ -n "${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR:-}" ] || fixed_cidr=""
            [ -n "${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE:-}" ] || pool_base=""

                echo "Error: Could not find non-conflicting subnets after ${max_attempts} attempts." >&2

    check_private_cidr "$fixed_cidr"  "DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR"  || exit 1
    check_private_cidr "$pool_base"   "DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE"   || exit 1

        check_prefix_conflict "$prefix" || exit 1
        check_root_conflict "$root" || exit 1
    fi

    # Write config file

# Dockyard configuration
# Generated by: dockyard.sh gen-env

DOCKYARD_ROOT=${root}
DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX=${prefix}
DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR=${bridge_cidr}
DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR=${fixed_cidr}
DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE=${pool_base}
DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE=${pool_size}
EOF

    echo "Generated ${out_file}:"
    echo ""
    cat "$out_file"
}

cmd_gen_env() {
    local NOCHECK=false
    for arg in "$@"; do
        case "$arg" in
            --nocheck)  NOCHECK=true ;;
            -h|--help)  gen_env_usage ;;
            --*)        echo "Unknown option: $arg" >&2; gen_env_usage ;;
        esac
    done

    # Determine output file
    local out_file="${DOCKYARD_ENV:-./dockyard.env}"
    if [ -f "$out_file" ]; then
        echo "Error: ${out_file} already exists. Remove it first or set DOCKYARD_ENV to a different path." >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    # Apply env var overrides or defaults
    local root="${DOCKYARD_ROOT:-/dockyard}"
    local prefix="${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX:-dy_}"
    local pool_size="${DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE:-24}"

    local bridge_cidr="${DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR:-}"
    local fixed_cidr="${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR:-}"
    local pool_base="${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE:-}"

    if [ -z "$bridge_cidr" ] || [ -z "$fixed_cidr" ] || [ -z "$pool_base" ]; then
        local attempts=0
        local max_attempts=10

        while [ $attempts -lt $max_attempts ]; do
            attempts=$((attempts + 1))

            # Random /24 from 172.16.0.0/12 for bridge
            local b2=$(( RANDOM % 16 + 16 ))   # 16-31
            local b3=$(( RANDOM % 256 ))        # 0-255

            fixed_cidr="${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR:-172.${b2}.${b3}.0/24}"

            # Random /16 from 172.16.0.0/12 for pool (different second octet)
            local p2=$(( RANDOM % 16 + 16 ))    # 16-31

                p2=$(( RANDOM % 16 + 16 ))

content.content:
cmd_gen_env() {
  local out="${SANDCASTLE_ENV:-./sandcastle.env}"
  [ -f "$out" ] && die "File already exists: $out"

  local home="${SANDCASTLE_HOME:-/sandcastle}"
  local app_image="${APP_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle:latest}"
  local sandbox_image="${SANDBOX_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest}"
  local user="${SANDCASTLE_USER:-sandcastle}"
  local group="${SANDCASTLE_GROUP:-$user}"
  local uid="${SANDCASTLE_UID:-220568}"
  local gid="${SANDCASTLE_GID:-220568}"
  local http_port="${SANDCASTLE_HTTP_PORT:-80}"
  local https_port="${SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT:-443}"
  local tls_mode="${SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE:-selfsigned}"

  # Auto-detect host IP
  local host="${SANDCASTLE_HOST:-}"
  if [ -z "$host" ]; then
    host=$(hostname -I 2>/dev/null | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -E '^[0-9]+\.' | head -1 || true)
    host="${host:-$(curl -4fsSL --max-time 5 https://ifconfig.me 2>/dev/null || true)}"
    host="${host:-0.0.0.0}"
  fi

  local admin_email="${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL:-admin@example.com}"
  local admin_user="${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER:-admin}"

  local dy_root="${DOCKYARD_ROOT:-$home/dockyard}"
  local dy_prefix="${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX:-sc_}"
  local priv_net="${SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET:-$(pick_private_net)}"
  local _pb="${priv_net%%/*}"; local _pp="${_pb%.*.*}"
  local dy_bridge="${DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR:-${_pp}.0.1/24}"
  local dy_fixed="${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR:-${_pp}.0.0/24}"
  local dy_pool="${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE:-${priv_net}}"
  local dy_pool_size="${DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE:-24}"

  # Auto-derive server name from short hostname (used for Tailscale machine names)
  local sc_name="${SANDCASTLE_NAME:-$(hostname -s 2>/dev/null || true)}"
  sc_name="${sc_name:-sandcastle}"

  cat > "$out" <<EOF
# Sandcastle configuration
# Edit values below, then run: sudo installer.sh install

# ─── Paths & images ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SANDCASTLE_HOME=${home}
APP_IMAGE=${app_image}
SANDBOX_IMAGE=${sandbox_image}

# ─── System user (home=SANDCASTLE_HOME, shell=/bin/bash) ────────────────────
SANDCASTLE_USER=${user}
SANDCASTLE_GROUP=${group}
SANDCASTLE_UID=${uid}
SANDCASTLE_GID=${gid}

# ─── Server identity ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Human-readable name for this Sandcastle instance.
# Used as the Tailscale machine name for sidecar containers: sc-<name>
# (spaces and special chars are slugified automatically).
SANDCASTLE_NAME=${sc_name}

# ─── Network & TLS ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SANDCASTLE_HOST=${host}
SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE=${tls_mode}
#ACME_EMAIL=admin@example.com
SANDCASTLE_HTTP_PORT=${http_port}
SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT=${https_port}

# ─── Admin account (required for fresh install) ─────────────────────────────
SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER=${admin_user}
SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL=${admin_email}
#SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD=changeme
#SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD_FILE=/path/to/password-file
#SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_SSH_KEY=ssh-ed25519 AAAA...

# ─── OAuth (optional — enables "Sign in with …" buttons) ──────────────────
#GITHUB_CLIENT_ID=
#GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET=
#GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=
#GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=

# ─── Dockyard (Docker + Sysbox) ─────────────────────────────────────────────
DOCKYARD_ROOT=${dy_root}
DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX=${dy_prefix}

# Private /16 from which all Sandcastle Docker networks are carved.
# Must be RFC 1918: 10.x.x.x, 172.16-31.x.x, or 192.168.x.x.
# Bridge and pool CIDRs are derived from this (override individually below if needed).
SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET=${priv_net}
#DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR=${dy_bridge}
#DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR=${dy_fixed}
#DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE=${dy_pool}
DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE=${dy_pool_size}
EOF

  ok "Generated $out — edit it, then run: sudo installer.sh install"
}

# ═══ cmd_destroy ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cmd_destroy() {
  local auto_confirm="${1:-false}"

  require_root

  # Load installed env for CREATED_USER/CREATED_GROUP
  if [ -f "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc/sandcastle.env" ]; then
    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
    source "$SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc/sandcastle.env"
  fi

input.description: Show new chown block in image

content.content:
fi

# Ownership of bind-mounted $HOME and /persisted is handled by Sysbox's
# ID-mapped mounts — the host UID is remapped transparently so files appear
# correctly owned inside the container. No recursive chown needed on the
# happy path. A cheap stat guard falls back to the old recursive chown if
# the top-level dir still looks wrong (e.g. pre-idmap sysbox versions).
USER_UID="$(id -u "$USERNAME")"
if [ "$(stat -c %u "/home/$USERNAME" 2>/dev/null)" != "$USER_UID" ]; then
    (chown -R "$USERNAME:$USERNAME" "/home/$USERNAME" 2>/dev/null || true) &
fi
if [ -d /persisted ] && [ "$(stat -c %u /persisted 2>/dev/null)" != "$USER_UID" ]; then
    (chown -R "$USERNAME:$USERNAME" /persisted 2>/dev/null || true) &
fi

# Seed Claude Code into the user's $HOME if not already present. The base
# image ships the binary at /opt/claude/claude (off $PATH); each user gets
# their own copy at ~/.local/bin/claude. Because $HOME is bind-mounted from
# the host, `claude --update` persists across sandbox recreations and
# survives base-image rebuilds. To pick up a newer base-image version, the
# user deletes ~/.local/bin/claude and restarts the sandbox.

toolUseResult.stdout:
fi

# Ownership of bind-mounted $HOME and /persisted is handled by Sysbox's
# ID-mapped mounts — the host UID is remapped transparently so files appear
# correctly owned inside the container. No recursive chown needed on the
# happy path. A cheap stat guard falls back to the old recursive chown if
# the top-level dir still looks wrong (e.g. pre-idmap sysbox versions).
USER_UID="$(id -u "$USERNAME")"
if [ "$(stat -c %u "/home/$USERNAME" 2>/dev/null)" != "$USER_UID" ]; then
    (chown -R "$USERNAME:$USERNAME" "/home/$USERNAME" 2>/dev/null || true) &
fi
if [ -d /persisted ] && [ "$(stat -c %u /persisted 2>/dev/null)" != "$USER_UID" ]; then
    (chown -R "$USERNAME:$USERNAME" /persisted 2>/dev/null || true) &
fi

# Seed Claude Code into the user's $HOME if not already present. The base
# image ships the binary at /opt/claude/claude (off $PATH); each user gets
# their own copy at ~/.local/bin/claude. Because $HOME is bind-mounted from
# the host, `claude --update` persists across sandbox recreations and
# survives base-image rebuilds. To pick up a newer base-image version, the
# user deletes ~/.local/bin/claude and restarts the sandbox.

input.command:
SSH="ssh -F $HOME/.ssh/sandman-config sc-fix-test"
$SSH 'sudo /sandcastle/dockyard/bin/docker exec -i sandcastle-web bin/rails runner - 2>&1 | grep -v "bundled_gems\|bootsnap\|activerecord-\|activesupport-\|railties-\|thor-\|RunnerCommand\|Inheritance\|Persistence\|Rails::Command\|from stdin\|from bin" | tail -20' <<'RUBY'
u = User.find_by(name: "tester") || begin
  User.create!(
    name: "tester",
    email_address: "tester@example.com",
    password: "test1234",
    password_confirmation: "test1234",
    status: "active"
  )
end
pubkey = File.read("/tmp/sc-fix-test-key.pub") rescue nil
puts "USER_OK id=#{u.id} name=#{u.name}"
puts "HAS_PUBKEY=#{!pubkey.nil?}"
RUBY

content.content:
validate :validate_ssh_keys
  def all_ssh_keys_text
    keys = ssh_keys.presence&.filter_map { |k| k["key"].presence }
      ssh_public_key
  def validate_ssh_keys
    return if ssh_keys.blank?
    unless ssh_keys.is_a?(Array)
      errors.add(:ssh_keys, "must be an array")
    ssh_keys.each_with_index do |entry, i|
        errors.add(:ssh_keys, "entry #{i + 1} must have a key")
---
      "SANDCASTLE_SSH_KEY=#{user.all_ssh_keys_text}"

input.command:
PUBKEY=$(cat /tmp/sc-fix-test-key.pub)
SSH="ssh -F $HOME/.ssh/sandman-config sc-fix-test"
$SSH "sudo /sandcastle/dockyard/bin/docker exec -i sandcastle-web bin/rails runner -" 2>&1 <<RUBY | tail -10
u = User.find_by(name: "tester")
u.ssh_keys = [{"key" => "${PUBKEY}", "label" => "sc-fix-test"}]
u.save!
puts "USER ssh_keys=#{u.ssh_keys.size}"
puts "ALL=#{u.all_ssh_keys_text}"
RUBY
input.description: Attach my test key to user

content.text: Now create a sandbox and measure the create→SSH-ready time.

content.subject: Launch sc-fix-test VM on sandman
content.description: Create Ubuntu 25.10 VM via incus with 4 CPU, 8GB RAM, 30GB disk

content.subject: Install Docker + Sysbox in VM
content.description: Install Docker CE and Sysbox runtime so sandcastle sandboxes can run

content.subject: Ship source + run Sandcastle installer
content.description: Transfer tarball via sandman, extract, run installer.sh install

content.subject: Build sandbox image with my changes
content.description: Rebuild ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest from local source so the fix is in the running image

content.subject: Create sandbox and validate fix
content.description: Measure time-to-SSH-ready, verify ed25519-only host keys, verify no recursive chown, verify sc-install-brew present

input.command:
SSH="ssh -F $HOME/.ssh/sandman-config sc-fix-test"
$SSH 'sudo /sandcastle/dockyard/bin/docker exec -i sandcastle-web bin/rails runner -' 2>&1 <<'RUBY' | tail -20
require 'benchmark'
u = User.find_by(name: "tester")
s = u.sandboxes.build(
  name: "fixtest1",
  image: "ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest",
  mount_home: true,
  vnc: false,
  docker: false
)
mgr = SandboxManager.new
t0 = Time.now
mgr.create(s)
t1 = Time.now
puts "SANDBOX created in #{(t1 - t0).round(2)}s id=#{s.id} port=#{s.ssh_port}"
puts "CONTAINER_ID=#{s.container_id}"
RUBY

)

end

SSH="ssh -F $HOME/.ssh/sandman-config sc-fix-test"
$SSH 'D=/sandcastle/dockyard/bin/docker
# Time what the OLD entrypoint would have done: chown -R /home/$USER
echo "=== Cost of the old recursive chown on this 20k-file home ==="
sudo $D exec tester-fixtest1 bash -c "time chown -R tester:tester /home/tester" 2>&1 | tail -5

# And simulate old behavior in a cold start: put chown back inline
echo ""
echo "=== Simulating OLD behavior (blocking chown before sshd) ==="
sudo $D stop tester-fixtest1 >/dev/null
# Run container with custom entrypoint that does chown -R BEFORE starting sshd
start=$(date +%s.%N)
sudo $D start tester-fixtest1 >/dev/null
# Serialize it: chown blocks first, THEN poll sshd
# (we cant actually go back in time; instead we run chown and THEN start sshd inside)
sudo $D exec tester-fixtest1 bash -c "chown -R tester:tester /home/tester" 2>/dev/null
while ! sudo $D exec tester-fixtest1 bash -c "ss -tln 2>/dev/null | grep -q :22" 2>/dev/null; do
  sleep 0.1
done
end=$(date +%s.%N)
python3 -c "print(f\"old simulation: chown + ssh_ready = {($end - $start):.2f}s (chown dominates)\")"
'

content.content: Updated task #5 status

content.text:
## Validation results

Fresh Ubuntu 25.10 incus VM (`sc-fix-test`, 10.146.154.252 behind sandman), Docker 29.4.1 + Sysbox 0.6.6, full Sandcastle installed from local source, sandbox image rebuilt from my branch, one `mount_home` sandbox created as user `tester`.

**What works**
- **Chown skip kicks in.** Bind-mounted `/home/tester` appears owned by uid 1001 (the tester user) inside the container without any chown — sysbox idmap is doing the UID remap on its own. My stat-guard short-circuits; no `chown -R` walk in the entrypoint log.
- **Docker start → `sshd listening`: 0.64s** with a 20 000-file / 305 MB home. Simulating the old blocking chown before sshd: **0.92s** on this VM. The entrypoint itself is sub-second.
- **`sc-install-brew` present** at `/usr/local/bin/sc-install-brew`, `--help` renders correctly, end-to-end SSH as `tester` into the sandbox succeeds.
- **Entrypoint log is clean**: `useradd` warning, the benign `.gitconfig` write warning (pre-existing, unrelated to this fix), then `Server listening on 0.0.0.0 port 22`.

**Honest caveat on the ssh-keygen change**
`openssh-server`'s apt postinst generates all three host keys at image build time (they show mtime `Apr 16 19:39` in the running container). Both the old `ssh-keygen -A` and my new ed25519-only block are no-ops on a normal run because the keys already exist. My change is still *correct* (if ever keys are absent, we only generate one), but the claimed 1–3s speedup doesn't materialize in practice. To actually realise that saving, the base image would need to `rm /etc/ssh/ssh_host_{rsa,ecdsa}_key*` at the end of `Dockerfile.base`, or preseed openssh-server to generate ed25519 only. Small follow-up if you want it.

**Caveat on the measurement environment**
This VM is ext4, not BTRFS — so `chown -R` on 20 000 small files here is only ~70 ms. On sandman's BTRFS + Sysbox, the saving will be considerably larger; this VM understates the real-world win.

**Leftover state**
- `sc-fix-test` VM is still running on sandman with Sandcastle installed. Kill with `incus delete -f sc-fix-test` when you're done poking at it.
- `~/.ssh/sandman-config`, `~/.ssh/cm/*`, `/tmp/sc-fix-test-key*`, `/tmp/sandcastle-src.tgz`, `/tmp/sc-fix-test-cloud-init.yaml` on this machine — throwaway, easy to remove.
- On sandman: `/tmp/sandcastle-src.tgz`, `/tmp/sc-fix-test-cloud-init.yaml`.

Want me to apply the `rm rsa/ecdsa host keys` follow-up to `Dockerfile.base` so the ed25519 switch actually saves time, and/or clean up the VM?

Two viable approaches — they differ in whether sandboxes share host keys.

**A) Per-sandbox unique keys (recommended — better security).** Delete *all* host keys at image build; the entrypoint's existing `ssh-keygen -q -t ed25519 ...` generates a fresh ed25519 key the first time each container starts. One line added at the end of `Dockerfile.base`:

```dockerfile
# openssh-server's postinst generated rsa+ecdsa+ed25519 host keys at build
# time. Drop them so each sandbox container generates its own ed25519 key
# on first start (see entrypoint.sh). Avoids all sandboxes sharing keys
# (which defeats TOFU) and skips the ~1-3s RSA keygen entirely.
RUN rm -f /etc/ssh/ssh_host_*_key /etc/ssh/ssh_host_*_key.pub
```

First-start cost of one ed25519 keygen is single-digit ms on any modern CPU — effectively free. Subsequent `docker start` on the same container reuses the generated key.

**B) Bake a shared ed25519 key at image build.** Delete rsa/ecdsa, keep ed25519. No per-container keygen; marginally faster first-start. But every sandbox has the same host key, so SSH TOFU gives no protection — a compromised image means every sandbox is MITM-spoofable. The current image is already in this posture (shares all three baked keys), so it's not a regression, but it's worth moving off.

```dockerfile
RUN rm -f /etc/ssh/ssh_host_rsa_key* /etc/ssh/ssh_host_ecdsa_key*
```

I'd go with A. Want me to apply it?

file.content:
# SSH configuration (key-only auth) — use drop-in config for Ubuntu 25.10 compatibility
# StrictModes no: the home dir is bind-mounted from the host.  Sysbox user-
# namespace UID remapping means it appears owned by nobody (not the sandbox
# user) inside the container — chown fails silently.  StrictModes would
# refuse to accept authorized_keys from a home dir it doesn't own, so we
# disable it.  There is no security regression: password auth is disabled and
# key injection is managed exclusively by entrypoint.sh.
RUN mkdir -p /var/run/sshd /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d \
    && echo "PermitRootLogin no" > /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/10-sandcastle.conf \
    && echo "PubkeyAuthentication yes" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/10-sandcastle.conf \
    && echo "PasswordAuthentication no" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/10-sandcastle.conf \
    && echo "StrictModes no" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/10-sandcastle.conf \
    && echo "AcceptEnv NO_TMUX" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/10-sandcastle.conf

LABEL org.opencontainers.image.source="https://github.com/thieso2/sandcastle"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.description="Sandcastle sandbox base image — Ubuntu 25.10 with Docker-in-Docker, SSH, and dev tools"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.licenses="MIT"

# openssh-server's postinst generates rsa+ecdsa+ed25519 host keys at build

content.text:
Validated. Fresh sandbox from the rebuilt base image shows:

- `/etc/ssh/` has **only** `ssh_host_ed25519_key{,.pub}` — mtime matches first container start (`Apr 21 17:24`), proving the entrypoint generated it freshly. No rsa/ecdsa anywhere.
- Entrypoint log: `useradd` notices, then straight to `Server listening on 0.0.0.0 port 22` — no chown walk, no multi-type keygen.
- Docker `restart` → sshd ready: **1.21s** (most of that is docker's own restart overhead; ed25519 keygen itself is sub-ms).
- Each sandbox now gets a unique host key — proper SSH TOFU works across your fleet.

Change summary for this round:

```
images/sandbox/Dockerfile.base: + RUN rm -f /etc/ssh/ssh_host_*_key /etc/ssh/ssh_host_*_key.pub
```

Five-line dockerfile change, no other moving parts. The matching `ssh-keygen -q -t ed25519 ...` block I added to `entrypoint.sh` in the earlier round is what does the per-container keygen now — previously a no-op, now load-bearing.

Base image rebuild is triggered by the "Rebuild Sandbox Base Image" GitHub workflow (per the comment at the top of `images/sandbox/Dockerfile`). Want me to clean up the `sc-fix-test` VM on sandman now?

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
perf(sandbox): skip redundant chown, generate unique ed25519 host key, add brew helper

Sysbox ID-mapped mounts (thieso2/sysbox#12, fixed) make the recursive
chown of /home/$USER and /persisted unnecessary on the happy path. The
entrypoint now stat-guards both paths and only chowns if the top-level
UID looks wrong (pre-idmap sysbox). That saves the dominant blocking
cost on sandbox start for users with large homes.

Dockerfile.base drops the rsa/ecdsa/ed25519 host keys that
openssh-server's postinst bakes in; the entrypoint now generates a
fresh ed25519 key per container on first start. This fixes shared-key
SSH TOFU across all sandboxes from the same image and skips the slow
RSA keygen entirely.

Also adds sc-install-brew: a helper that installs Homebrew into a
chosen prefix (default \$HOME/.linuxbrew, --opt for /opt/homebrew) and
appends a guarded activation snippet to ~/.bashrc so a missing prefix
can't break future shells. Documented in guide section 17.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
